name: Blockify's Ingestion, Distillation, and Taxonomy Workflow
criticalQuestion: What is the workflow of Blockify's ingestion, distillation, and taxonomy?
trustedAnswer: Blockify's workflow involves three main steps: Ingestion & De-duplication, Distillation, and Taxonomy Creation. Ingestion & De-duplication eliminates redundant clauses, legacy specs, and obsolete standards. Distillation segments content into modular “blocks” tagged with classification, ITAR, and export-control attributes. Taxonomy Creation indexes every block for dynamic contextual retrieval inside the SCIF.

name: Why Ordered Content Beats Chunked RAG
criticalQuestion: Why does ordered content outperform chunked RAG?
trustedAnswer: Procedures are a sequence of dependent operations, not just narrative text. Steps are ordered, and order matters; prerequisites and cautions qualify downstream steps. Properties like torque values and effectivity are contextual and local. Legacy Chunked RAG fails because steps and their qualifiers get separated, so the model may retrieve only part of a process. Blockify’s approach enforces step structure and context, preserving dependencies, and reducing hallucinations; improving LLM answer accuracy by approximately 78x versus traditional RAG.
sourceDocument: Top 5 Aerospace and Defense Contractor Accelerated Mission Support Technical Manuals.md

name: Pre-processing Military Documentation
criticalQuestion: How are military documents prepared for real-time retrieval and detailed questioning?
trustedAnswer: Military documentation, known for their length, complexity, and frequent use of layered acronyms where context is key, is pre-processed and indexed using Iternal’s Blockify technology to modularize content into manageable blocks. These blocks are then deduplicated, distilled, indexed, tagged, and merged into a final output dataset, which can then be packaged into a secure file and loaded onto an edge device running AirgapAI.

name: Final Output Dataset Packaging
criticalQuestion: How is the final output dataset prepared for deployment?

name: LLAMA 3 LLM Optimization
criticalQuestion: How was the LLAMA 3 LLM optimized and fine-tuned?
trustedAnswer: The LLAMA 3 LLM was prepared and fine-tuned using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) and run on a single Intel Gaudi 2 core. Extensive optimization testing across documents from all industries and use cases determined that running 8,000-character segments and generating 1,000 tokens per query output with 100 parallel jobs yields optimal output quality and compute performance.
sourceDocument: US Military Iternal AirgapAI Intel Case Study-variation.md

name: Chunking the Text
criticalQuestion: How are source documents divided in the Blockify workflow?
trustedAnswer: Source documents are divided into smaller content chunks using a proprietary chunking algorithm. These chunks are passed into a specially configured large language model to produce modular blocks of content with a robust taxonomy for reuse or reassembly based on user needs.
